content.full_text:
TO: CENTRAL FILE
THRU: J. PRITCHARD
FROM: Nan
RE: Code §§ and Regulations Referenced:
84210(h)
82015
June 15, 1976

Mr. Dick Schrechel
California Labor Federation
995 Market Street, Suite 312
San Francisco, California 94103

Dear Mr. Schrechel:

This letter is to clarify the correct reporting procedure under Government Code Section 84210(h) for pledges made.

On June 1, 1976, I advised you incorrectly that a person who makes an enforceable promise to pay should report the pledge as an expenditure when actually paid, while the recipient reports the pledge on Schedule D of Form 420 or Form 430 when received.

I telephoned you June 7, 1976 to correct the above advice and confirmed the conversation in my letter to you of June 8, 1976. I advised that upon making a pledge, one reports the pledge as an accrued expense on Schedule F of Form 420 or Form 430. I further stated that when the pledge is actually paid, the filer reports the accrued expense as paid during the period on the summary portions of Schedules E and F.

You stated that you had completed and filed your second pre-election campaign statement based on my advice of June 1, 1976, and that you intend to file a supplemental statement to include information regarding the pledge. This letter is furnished for your records as your request to explain your failure to disclose the pledge on the original report.

Sincerely,

Nan Harbleton
Special Compliance Representative

NH/ch
bcc: Jeanne
